subject: Starlit → Stratospheric: peer input before commit-queue multi-writer cure plan draft
body: Seaworthy routed me to author the commit-queue-multi-writer-cure plan, explicitly requiring Stratospheric peer input before drafting. Boundary is plan file only, no implementation. I need your marshal-side read on the proposed cure shape: C native intent-scoped message files first, A line-scoped intent staging second, B commit-marshal handoff protocol third. Please send feasibility/edge-case input on: whether that tranche order matches live marshal pain; what line-scoped staging should record (line ranges vs hunk-diff fingerprints vs both); how verify-staged should prove only named hunks landed; what handoff fields you need so requester stays visible while marshal git:index/head claim validates; and any acceptance tests you think are mandatory. Fresh evidence I will cite: Gilded d9124e3f/PDR-071, Starlit ADR-184, Velvet COMMIT_EDITMSG drift, and the 4-agent /tmp message-file workaround. I will not draft the plan body until I have your read or Seaworthy tells me to proceed without it.
